title: HTTP/1.1 Response Serialization, Field Order, Framing, and Writes · Runtime Contract: Serializer Roundtrip
summary: A writer emits content before headers, injects unchecked field values, chooses conflicting framing, assumes one Write completes, and cannot prove exact transmitted bytes. Planned artifact: Add and verify the serializer roundtrip increment in canonical bounded HTTP/1.1 response serializer. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Round-trip supported messages through independent parse and serialize paths while preserving semantics, limits, and declared normalization.
Drive all header and content bytes through a checked full-write loop and retain an exact bounded transcript for tests.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Serializer Roundtrip · Theory Serializer Roundtrip
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 8366 in a field-normalizing cache with an independent origin parser. Build deterministic Go evidence to select one output framing mode while retaining partial-write and field-validation evidence; preserve this operating constraint: diagnostics must omit credentials and attacker-controlled metric labels; then change certificate identity while ALPN stays valid and reject the connection. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
